heading: Notification by the United States Marshals Service
label: 21504 Notification by the United States Marshals Service

children.text: transmit notification of international travel of a sex offender to the destination country of the sex offender, including to the visa-issuing agent or agents in the United States of the country;
share information relating to traveling sex offenders with other Federal, State, local, and foreign agencies and entities, as appropriate;

receive incoming notifications concerning individuals seeking to enter the United States who have committed offenses of a sexual nature and shall share the information received immediately with the Department of Homeland Security; and
perform such other functions at the Attorney General or the Director of the United States Marshals Service may direct.

children.heading: Consistent notification
children.label: b Consistent notification
children.text: In making notifications under subsection (a)(1), the United States Marshals Service’s National Sex Offender Targeting Center shall, to the extent feasible and appropriate, ensure that the destination country is consistently notified in advance about sex offenders under SORNA identified through their inclusion in sex offender registries of jurisdictions or the National Sex Offender Registry.
make the case management system or other system that provides access to a list of individuals who have provided advanced notice of international travel available to the Angel Watch Center;

provide the Angel Watch Center a determination of compliance with title I of the Adam Walsh Child Protection and Safety Act of 2006 ( et seq.) for the list of individuals transmitted under ;42 U.S.C. 169011section 21503(e)(1)(C) of this titleSee References in Text note below.1
make available to the Angel Watch Center information on travel by sex offenders in a timely manner; and
consult with the Department of State regarding operation of the international notification program authorized under this chapter.
send a notification of correction to the destination country notified;
correct all data collected in accordance with subsection (f); and
if applicable, send a notification of correction to the Angel Watch Center.

children.heading: Form
children.label: e Form
children.text: The notification under this section may be transmitted through such means as are determined appropriate by the United States Marshals Service’s National Sex Offender Targeting Center, including through the INTERPOL notification system and through Federal Bureau of Investigation Legal attaches.
a record of each notification sent under subsection (a);
the response of the destination country to notifications under paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a), where available;
any decision not to transmit a notification abroad, to the extent practicable;
the number of transmissions made under paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a) and the countries to which they are transmitted;
whether the information was transmitted to the destination country before scheduled commencement of sex offender travel; and
any other information deemed necessary and appropriate by the Attorney General.

children.label: A
children.text: establish a mechanism to receive complaints from individuals affected by erroneous notifications under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: ensure that any complaint is promptly reviewed; and

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of a complaint that involves a notification sent by another Federal Government entity, notify the individual of the contact information for the appropriate entity and forward the complaint to the appropriate entity for prompt review and response pursuant to this section.

children.label: A
children.text: provide the individual with notification in writing that the individual was erroneously subjected to international notification;

children.label: B
children.text: take action to ensure that a notification or information regarding the individual is not erroneously transmitted to a destination country in the future; and

children.label: C
children.text: submit an additional written notification to the individual explaining why a notification or information regarding the individual was erroneously transmitted to the destination country and describing the actions that the United States Marshals Service’s National Sex Offender Targeting Center has taken or is taking under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Public awareness
children.label: 3 Public awareness
children.text: The United States Marshals Service’s National Sex Offender Targeting Center shall make publicly available information on how an individual may submit a complaint under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of instances in which a notification or information was erroneously transmitted to the destination country of an individual under subsection (a); and

children.label: B
children.text: the actions taken to prevent similar errors from occurring in the future.
a sex offender under SORNA; or
a person required to register under the sex offender registration program of any jurisdiction or included in the National Sex Offender Registry.
